_note: Single source of truth for all numeric claims used across multiple documents. Update values HERE; run ci/sync_canonical_numbers.py to propagate. Agents must never hand-edit README.md / PR_DESCRIPTION.md / BACKEND_EVIDENCE.md numbers directly — always update this file first.
_generated: 2026-05-11 — verified by fresh controlled run (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1, governor=performance, taskset -c 0, nice -20, GCC 14.2.0)

bitcoin_core_compat.note: 749/749 is the current GCC 14 run (docs/BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json). An earlier Clang 19 run against v28.0 showed 693/693 (docs/BITCOIN_CORE_TEST_RESULTS.json).

connectblock.turbo_lock_status: CONFIRMED: cpupower frequency-set -g performance + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1 (2026-05-12 hard-turbo-locked rerun)
connectblock.wording: ConnectBlock aggregate block validation: +0.9–1.5% faster than libsecp256k1 with Release+LTO (GCC 14.2.0, hard turbo lock, 5 runs, err% 0.1–0.5%). Without LTO: ~0.5–1.0% slower (i-cache pressure; PERF-002 reduced gap from ~1.1% but did not eliminate it). LTO is required for Ultra to win all ConnectBlock workloads.
connectblock.wording_no_lto: Without LTO: ~0.5–1.0% slower than libsecp256k1 due to i-cache pressure from Ultra's larger code footprint (~1.3 MB vs libsecp ~400 KB). PERF-002 reduced the gap from ~1.1% but did not fully eliminate it. Use Release+LTO for production Bitcoin Core builds.

taproot_signing.wording: Taproot signing: 10–35% faster than libsecp256k1 (SignTransactionSchnorr 1.10×; SignSchnorrWithMerkleRoot 1.35×, GCC 14.2.0, Release+LTO, hard turbo lock, 2026-05-12, docs/BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json).

taproot_verify.wording: P2TR script-path verification is ~10% faster (1.10×, VerifyScriptP2TR_ScriptPath, GCC 14.2.0 LTO).

schnorr_sign_merkle.wording: SignSchnorrWithMerkleRoot is ~35% faster (1.35×, GCC 14.2.0 LTO, hard turbo lock, docs/BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json).

ct_signing_clang19.wording: CT signing Clang 19 (archived 2026-03-24): 1.20–1.33× faster than libsecp256k1 (CT-vs-CT, not a current controlled run).

ct_signing_gcc.wording: CT signing GCC 14.2.0: 1.09–1.24× faster than libsecp256k1 (CT Schnorr 1.09×; CT ECDSA 1.24×; CT-vs-CT, bench_unified_2026-05-11_gcc14_x86-64.json).
